{{Quote|Yesterday, an Alliance surveillance craft was shot down by an Imperial vessel over an unexplored world, NCW-781.|Rebel base commander|<ref name="IA" />}}
'''NCW-781''' was an [[Scout/Legends|unexplored]] [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. A terrestrial world, its surface featured [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]], canyons, and [[Forest/Legends|woods]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] operative [[Kala Kevv]] was shot down over NCW-781, where he crash-landed while carrying a [[Prodigal-Two|data plaque]] with information vital to the Alliance.

The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] dispatched a [[Imperial Military/Legends|force]] to the planet to locate the Rebel agent, and the Alliance [[Operation: Shadowstrike|sent]] a [["Shadowstrike" team|team]] of [[Alliance Special Forces/Legends|Special Forces]] [[Infiltrators/Legends|Infiltrators]] to recover and extract Kevv and the [[Dataplaque/Legends|data plaque]]. Arriving at NCW-781 aboard the [[Light freighter/Legends|light freighter]] ''[[Bravado]]'', the team descended to the planet's surface on [[R-19 Paraglider|combat paragliders]] and then glided through a canyon in order to approach Kevv's crash site undetected by the Imperials.

Passing by a [[Storm commando/Legends|storm commando]] patrol in the woods, the Infiltrators reached the crash site, from where a set of coordinates left by Kevv led the team to his hiding place. After recovering the operative and the data plaque, the Rebels proceeded to their designated extraction point, where they had to fight off an Imperial force, including a [[HAVr A9 Floating Fortress/Legends|Floating Fortress]] [[Repulsorcraft/Legends|repulsorcraft]], before they were extracted by the ''Bravado''.

==Description==
{{Quote|Prodigal One has likely abandoned the crash site, and has holed up in the nearby canyons.|Rebel base commander|<ref name="IA" />}}
NCW-781<ref name="IA" /> was a [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]]<ref name="EAOC" /> [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="IA" /> located in the [[NCW-781 system/Legends|NCW-781 system]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] by at least one [[Moon/Legends|moon]], with several relatively large astronomical objects visible in the planet's [[Standard day/Legends|night]] [[Atmosphere/Legends|skies]]. The surface of the terrestrial NCW-781 featured a range of [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]] and an adjacent system of canyons. Between the two lay an area covered by [[Forest/Legends|woods]], populated by [[Tree/Legends|trees]] and smaller [[Plant/Legends|plants]]. The mountain range was also the site of a cluster of [[Cave/Legends|caves]].<ref name="IA" />

==History==
===An agent shot down===
{{Quote|Your mission is to locate Prodigal One and recover him and the data plaque. After recovering the targets, proceed to the extraction site we've selected.|Rebel base commander, to the "Shadowstrike" team|<ref name="IA" />}}
[[File:KalaKevv-IA.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Kala Kevv was shot down and crash-landed on NCW-781.]]
At some point during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], an Imperial [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Space warfare/Legends|shot down]] an Alliance surveillance [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] by the [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] Rebel operative [[Kala Kevv]], who was carrying a [[Prodigal-Two|data plaque]] containing the identities of Imperial double agents. The confrontation took place over NCW-781, which at the [[Time/Legends|time]] remained [[Scout/Legends|unexplored]]. Kevv's [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] subsequently crashed on the planet's surface, and the Rebel agent sent out a [[Distress signal/Legends|distress call]].<ref name="IA" />

Shortly thereafter, an Imperial [[Strike-class medium cruiser|''Strike''-class cruiser]] dispatched a [[Imperial Military/Legends|ground force]] to the planet in order to find Kevv. The force included the [[NightWhispers]] unit of [[Storm commando/Legends|Imperial storm commandos]], [[CompForce Assault trooper]]s, a [[HAVr A9 Floating Fortress/Legends|Floating Fortress]] [[Repulsorcraft/Legends|repulsorcraft]], and a flight of [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE bombers]] used for surveillance. Not long after receiving Kevv's distress signal, the Rebel Alliance sent a [[Probe droid/Legends|probe droid]] to perform a reconnaissance fly-by of NCW-781, which allowed the Rebels to pinpoint the location of Kevv's crash site as well as learn of the presence of the Imperial forces.<ref name="IA" />

{{RPG|"[[Operation: Shadowstrike (adventure)|Operation: Shadowstrike]]"}}
===Infiltrators on NCW-781===
{{Quote|Get ready to hop out, team. We're about to enter the atmosphere.|The pilot of the ''Bravado'', to the "Shadowstrike" team|<ref name="IA" />}}
[[File:OperationShadowstrikeMap.png|thumb|right|220px|The Rebel Alliance codenamed several areas of NCW-781 in preparation for Operation: Shadowstrike.]]
Subsequently, the [[Unidentified Rebel commander (Operation: Shadowstrike)|commander]] of a [[Unidentified Rebel base (Operation: Shadowstrike)|Rebel base]] [[Operation: Shadowstrike|sent]] a [["Shadowstrike" team|team]] of [[Alliance Special Forces/Legends|Special Forces]] [[Infiltrators/Legends|Infiltrators]] to locate Kevv, code-named "[[Prodigal-One]]," and recover and extract both him and the [[Dataplaque/Legends|data plaque]] he was carrying, designated "Prodigal-Two." Given a limited time window to complete its mission, the Infiltrator team was transported to NCW-781 aboard the [[Light freighter/Legends|light freighter]] ''[[Bravado]]'', which, before settling into the planet's orbit, performed a high-atmospheric pass.<ref name="IA" />

While the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] was passing through a [[Cloud/Legends|cloud]] bank at an altitude of roughly 6,000 [[Meter/Legends|meters]], the "Shadowstrike" team exited its [[Transport/Legends|transport]] while equipped with [[R-19 Paraglider|combat paragliders]]. The Rebels descended to NCW-781's surface and glided along [[Approach Route Alpha]], a path through a canyon that would allow the team to reach Kevv's crash site undetected by Imperial surveillance. Ending the canyon run at a point designated [[Target Bantha]], the team then collected its equipment that had been air-dropped.<ref name="IA" />

===Extraction of Prodigal-One===
{{Quote|You've got a problem, team. There's something pretty substantial moving your way. Do you want to abort and proceed to Target Yavin or are we going for it? It's your call.|The ''Bravado'' pilot, to the "Shadowstrike" team|<ref name="IA" />}}
Continuing their trek on [[Limb/Legends|foot]], the Infiltrators encountered an Imperial storm [[Commando/Legends|commando]] patrol but remained undetected in the darkness of the night. The Rebels then reached [[Target Dewback]], Kevv's crash site, where they discovered that the agent had left encrypted coordinates at the wreck of his starfighter that led to his hiding place. The injured Sullustan had retreated to a cave in the foothills of the nearby mountains and had evaded storm commandos for [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]].<ref name="IA" />

[[File:NCW-781.png|thumb|left|220px|The "Shadowstrike" team fought Imperial forces on NCW-781.]]
The Infiltrators recovered Kevv and his data plaque but were spotted by Imperial forces and also notified by the [[Unidentified Rebel pilot (Bravado)|pilot]] of the ''Bravado'' of the unexpected arrival of the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Imperial Star Destroyer]] [[Rampage (Imperial-class)|''Rampage'']] in the [[Star system/Legends|star system]]. The Rebels proceeded expeditiously to their extraction area, codenamed [[Target Tallon]]. Reaching their destination, the team signaled the ''Bravado'' but then discovered that the Imperial Floating Fortress, accompanied by storm commandos and [[Assault (CompForce)/Legends|CompForce Assault]] [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]], was approaching their location. The Rebels fended off the Imperial force until the arrival of the ''Bravado'', which, under the cover of its [[Quad laser cannon/Legends|quad laser cannons]], extracted both the Infiltrators and Kevv.<ref name="IA" />
{{Endgame}}

==Locations==
{{Quote|As a result of the probe fly-by, we have identified three items of concern for this mission: the location of the crashed Y-wing, the presence of at least one unit of Imperial Storm Commandoes ''[sic]'', and the location of the Imperial's ''[sic]'' staging area…fifteen kilometers from the Y-wing crash site.|Rebel base commander|<ref name="IA" />}}
The Rebel agent Kala Kevv crash-landed his Y-wing starfighter near a wooded area of NCW-781, at a point subsequently codenamed "Target Dewback" by the Rebel Alliance. Kevv then retreated to a cave in the mountains to the north-east of the crash site. Subsequently, the Galactic Empire forces established a staging area fifteen [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] to the south of Target Dewback. When planning the mission to recover Kevv, the Alliance designated two extraction points, Target Tallon and [[Target Yavin]], both located to the north of Kevv's crash site, as well as Approach Route Alpha, which led through a narrow canyon up to a point designated "Target Bantha."<ref name="IA" />

==Behind the scenes==
NCW-781 served as the main setting of "[[Operation: Shadowstrike (adventure)|Operation: Shadowstrike]]," a [[roleplaying game]] adventure authored by [[Eric S. Trautmann]], illustrated by [[Paul Daly]], and included in the [[1997]] sourcebook ''[[Instant Adventures]]'' for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. This article assumes the scenario of the adventure plays out as described.<ref name="IA" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the NCW-781 system, and therefore NCW-781 itself, in grid square H-18.<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>
